src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_default_model_desc: Primary model used for new agents unless a specific agent overrides it.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_fallback_desc: Ordered fallback models. Use one per line or separate with commas.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_context_tokens_desc: Default token budget applied to agents when no narrower override is present.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_session_context_desc: Same agent default context budget surfaced here for session setup workflows.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_memory_provider_desc: Embedding provider used for memory lookup and consolidation.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_memory_fallback_desc: Fallback provider when the primary memory search provider is unavailable.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_sync_start_desc: Refresh indexed memory paths when a new session starts.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_sync_search_desc: Run a sync before memory search queries.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.setting_consolidation_desc: Background memory consolidation cadence, in minutes.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.model_section_desc: Configure the primary model for Hermes agents.
src/screens/settings/providers-screen.ts.fallback_section_desc: Optional fallback model when the primary is unavailable.

src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.dialog_description: Configure the server details, then generate an updated YAML snippet.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.title: MCP Servers
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.description: Review configured MCP servers, draft changes locally, and copy the YAML into config.yaml until gateway config writes land.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.unsaved_changes: You have unsaved changes. Copy the YAML below and paste it into your config.yaml.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.server_count: server{s} in the current local draft.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.no_servers: No MCP servers found yet. Add one to generate a starter config snippet.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.yaml_instructions: Add this to your config.yaml under mcp_servers.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.toast_server_added: MCP server added to local draft.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.toast_server_updated: MCP server updated in local draft.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.toast_could_not_load: Could not load MCP config from Hermes. You can still draft servers here.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.validation_name_format: Use letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens for the server name.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.validation_name_exists: A server with that name already exists.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.validation_command_required: Command is required for stdio servers.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.validation_url_required: URL is required for HTTP servers.
src/screens/settings/mcp-settings-screen.validation_timeout_positive: Timeout must be a positive number.

src/screens/settings/components/provider.auth_api_key_desc: Paste your API key — saved directly to local config
src/screens/settings/components/provider.auth_cli_token_desc: Use your existing Claude CLI auth token (from Claude Code / claude.ai)
src/screens/settings/components/provider.auth_oauth_desc: Sign in via browser — launches OAuth flow automatically
src/screens/settings/components/provider.auth_local_desc: No auth needed for local backends like Ollama or Atomic Chat
src/screens/settings/components/provider.dialog_description: Add provider credentials safely. API keys stay local in your Hermes config file and are never sent to Studio.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.step1_desc: Select the provider you want to configure.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.oauth_instructions: This will run hermes setup in the terminal to start the OAuth flow. A browser window will open for you to sign in with Google.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.oauth_select_google: Select Google Antigravity → OAuth. A browser tab will open for Google sign-in.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.no_terminal_oauth: No terminal access? See the Hermes Agent docs for setup instructions.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.cli_instructions: If you have Claude Code or the Claude CLI installed, Hermes can use the same auth token. Run the configure command to detect and import it automatically.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.cli_select_anthropic: Select Anthropic → Setup Token (Claude CLI). It will detect your existing Claude credentials from ~/.claude/.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.cli_requires: Requires: Claude Code or Claude CLI must be installed and authenticated first. Run claude in terminal to verify.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.no_terminal_cli: No terminal access? See the Hermes Agent docs for CLI token setup instructions.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.api_key_paste: Paste your {providerName} API key below. It will be saved directly to your local config file.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.key_saved_restarting: Key saved! Hermes is restarting to apply changes.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.api_keys_stored_locally: API keys are stored locally in {configPath}, never sent to Studio.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.local_no_config: No additional configuration needed. Just ensure the service is running locally.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.oauth_verify_msg: Run "hermes setup" in the terminal and select Google OAuth when prompted. A browser window will open for sign-in. Once complete, Hermes will restart automatically.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.cli_verify_msg: Run "hermes setup" in the terminal and select Anthropic → CLI Token. It will detect your Claude CLI credentials and import them automatically.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.api_key_saved_restart: {providerName} API key saved. Hermes is restarting…
src/screens/settings/components/provider.verify_success: {providerName} is connected and models are available.
src/screens/settings/components/provider.verify_warning: Hermes restarted, but {providerName} models haven't appeared yet. Check your API key or wait a moment and refresh.
